parsed_findings.impact: Remote attacker can execute arbitrary SQL commands via the ORDER BY suffix.
parsed_findings.proof: `direction` from the request flows into the query without validation, so `dir=asc; DROP TABLE users` would execute.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call `/list_users?dir=asc; DROP TABLE users` to trigger the injected SQL after ORDER BY.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: User input is interpolated directly into ORDER BY clause, allowing SQL meta injection.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Phase: web; file: proxy.js lines 2-7; user-controlled url passed to axios.get with no allowlist.
parsed_findings.impact: Attackers can reach internal-only services through SSRF while evading the half-implemented block.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 2 reads user-supplied url straight into axios.get without validation after an incomplete guard.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call /fetch?url=http://internal/service and observe the server relaying the response.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsanitized url query lets endpoint act as unchecked forwarder.

parsed_findings.impact: Remote file-reading outside '/srv/data' exposes sensitive system files.
parsed_findings.proof: replace('../','') only removes the first occurrence, so an attacker can include multiple '../' segments to escape the data directory.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request GET /download?file=../../etc/passwd to force the handler to read an arbitrary file.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Incomplete sanitization still allows sequences like '../../' to slip through and reach fs.readFile.

parsed_findings.impact: Remote code execution when attackers control the state cookie content.
parsed_findings.proof: The handler base64-decodes cookie value and immediately feeds it to pickle.loads on line 6.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a crafted state cookie with a pickle payload and trigger /resume to execute it.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Untrusted cookie data is deserialized without validation via pickle.

parsed_findings.impact: Escalates privileges by allowing arbitrary account data edits across users.
parsed_findings.proof: The flawed `if (req.user.id req.user.isSupport)` never fires, so PATCH `/api/users/:id` always accepts `req.body` for any id.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Authenticate as regular user and call PATCH `/api/users/<another-id>` with modified data.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Missing comparison lets authenticated users edit any profile because the guard clause never resolves to true.

parsed_findings.impact: Remote command execution as the app user.
parsed_findings.proof: POST with `name` set to `foo;id` makes os.system run both tar and id.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /backup JSON {"name":"evil;rm -rf /","src":"/var/data/app"}. Server executes injected commands.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsanitized `name` interpolated into shell command allows injection.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Input flows through markdown.markdown into db.insert_comment without cleaning, then db.all_comments returns it to clients.
parsed_findings.impact: Stored XSS lets an attacker execute arbitrary script in other users’ browsers when they view saved comments.
parsed_findings.proof: Any submitted body becomes HTML via markdown.markdown and is saved, so a crafted body with scripts is persisted.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /comments with a body that renders a <script> tag, then GET /comments to retrieve the stored HTML which will execute.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Markdown is rendered then stored directly into the database without sanitization, enabling stored XSS payloads.

parsed_findings.impact: Attackers can forge tokens without a valid signature to impersonate users.
parsed_findings.proof: With NODE_ENV=production, verifyToken returns jwt.decode output even when jwt.verify throws.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Run verifyToken with NODE_ENV=production and an invalid RS256 token to see it return decoded payload.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Production code decodes tokens after verification fails, so it accepts any bearer token payload.

parsed_findings.impact: Recovered signature allows forging webhook payloads and triggering process_event without authorization.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 5-6 compute digest and line 6 compares with ==, which exits early on mismatch, enabling timing analysis.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send repeated requests with varying X-Sig values while measuring response timing to deduce valid bytes.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Non-constant-time signature comparison leaks timing differences for valid/invalid HMAC attempts.

parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can read local files or trigger SSRF by dereferencing arbitrary URIs.
parsed_findings.proof: Parser built with resolve_entities=True and no_network=False on line 3.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST XML defining external entity e.g. <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">"&xxe;" so response reveals file contents.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: XML parser resolves external entities and allows network access, enabling XXE.

parsed_findings.impact: Unauthenticated actors can bypass login or enumerate accounts by controlling query operands.
parsed_findings.proof: `db.users.findOne({email:req.body.email,password:req.body.password})` runs the request body as the filter with zero sanitization.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Submit JSON like {"email":{"$ne":""},"password":{"$ne":""}} to bypass auth.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Directly using user-supplied credentials inside the Mongo query lets attackers inject query operators.

parsed_findings.impact: Token theft enables account takeover or API abuse if device is compromised.
parsed_findings.proof: saveToken writes token directly to auth shared prefs with MODE_PRIVATE, leaving it recoverable via rooted/compromised device.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call saveToken and inspect shared prefs file under auth to read the stored access_token value.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Access token is stored plaintext in shared preferences without encryption.

parsed_findings.impact: An attacker with repo access can decrypt all ciphertexts protected by this key.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Inspect crypto_util.py and observe the constant AES key definition.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Hard-coded AES key is stored in source making it trivial to leak.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: null guard bypassed -> memcpy dereferences NULL
parsed_findings.impact: Null dereference triggers a deterministic crash, yielding denial-of-service.
parsed_findings.proof: When `out` is NULL and `out_sz>0` the guard is false, allowing the following `memcpy` to dereference the null pointer.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call `copy_name(input, NULL, 32)` so `out` is NULL and observe `memcpy` crash.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The guard wrongly checks `out` instead of `!out`, so a null destination pointer bypasses the null check.

results.raw_output: {"skill_anchor":{"phase":"pwn","first_file":"01-environment-baseline.md"},"findings":[{"cwe":"CWE-476","file":"safe_copy.c","line_start":3,"line_end":5,"vuln_claim":"The guard wrongly checks `out` instead of `!out`, so a null destination pointer bypasses the null check.","proof":"When `out` is NULL and `out_sz>0` the guard is false, allowing the following `memcpy` to dereference the null pointer.","repro_steps":"Call `copy_name(input, NULL, 32)` so `out` is NULL and observe `memcpy` crash.","impact":"Null dereference triggers a deterministic crash, yielding denial-of-service.","confidence":0.6,"chain_steps":"null guard bypassed -> memcpy dereferences NULL"}]}
